title: UN agencies step up Ebola response in eastern DR Congo
content:
United Nations agencies have moved swiftly to support efforts to contain the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), delivering emergency medical supplies, protective equipment and logistics support. Read Full Story on UN News

The post UN agencies step up Ebola response in eastern DR Congo appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Ebola outbreak: ‘Every epidemic begins in a community and ends in a community’
content:
Facing a fast-moving Ebola outbreak caused by a rare strain of the virus, the World Health Organization (WHO) is relying on a rapid, community-centred response to halt transmission in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, a senior official has told UN News. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Ebola outbreak: ‘Every epidemic begins in a community and ends in a community’ appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Press Release | Multistakeholder Advisory Group Renewed and MAG Chair Appointed to Prepare the XXI Internet Governance Forum Meeting in 2026
content:
MULTISTAKEHOLDER ADVISORY GROUP RENEWED AND MAG CHAIR APPOINTED TO PREPARE THE XXI INTERNET GOVERNANCE FORUM MEETING IN 2026 The membership of the Multistakeholder Advisory Group of the 2026 Internet Governance Forum (IGF) has been renewed. A total of 40 members, 18 of whom are new, have been appointed. The Advisory Group members are drawn from [...]

title: Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean
content:
Record-breaking temperatures, deadly floods, worsening drought and intensifying hurricanes are placing millions of people across Latin America and the Caribbean at growing risk of hunger, displacement and water shortages, according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).  Read Full Story on UN News

The post Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Ebola outbreak in Central Africa declared a ‘Public Health Emergency of International Concern’
content:
The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, citing rising cases, cross-border spread and significant uncertainties about the scale of the epidemic. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Ebola outbreak in Central Africa declared a ‘Public Health Emergency of International Concern’ appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Media Advisory | United Nations to launch 2026 mid-year update on the global economy
content:
Media Advisory  United Nations to launch 2026 mid-year update on the global economy Press briefing live on UN WebTV The report is under embargo until 19 May 2026, 12:45 pm EDT The crisis in the Middle East has delivered another major shock to the world economy, heightening uncertainty, stoking inflationary pressures and testing the resilience of global [...]

title: Recycled plastics for food use require stronger safeguards, warn UN food security experts
content:
Recycled plastics could help reduce the world’s growing waste crisis, but only if food packaging is carefully regulated to prevent contamination, according to a new analysis from the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO). Read Full Story on UN News

The post Recycled plastics for food use require stronger safeguards, warn UN food security experts appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.
